stem: Increase $\$80$ by $15\%$. What is the new amount (in dollars)?
Decrease $\$200$ by $10\%$. What is the new amount (in dollars)?
A bag of sand weighs $450$ kg. Its weight increases by $20\%$. What is the new weight (in kg)?
Decrease $\$75$ by $40\%$. What is the new amount (in dollars)?
A worker earns $\$52{,}000$ per year. Their salary increases by $5\%$. What is their new annual salary (in dollars)?
A television is priced at $\$840$. It is reduced by $25\%$ in a sale. What is the sale price (in dollars)?
A town has a population of $12{,}400$. The population increases by $8\%$ over one year. What is the new population?
A jacket has a marked price of $\$180$. A store reduces its price by $35\%$. What is the new price (in dollars)?
After a $20\%$ discount, a pair of shoes costs $\$48$. What was the original price (in dollars)?
hint: A $20\%$ discount means the sale price is $80\%$ of the original. Divide by $0.80$.
explanation: Sale price $= 80\%$ of original, so original $= \$48 \div 0.80 = \$60$.

stem: After a $15\%$ price increase, an item costs $\$115$. What was the original price (in dollars)?
hint: A $15\%$ increase means the new price is $115\%$ of the original. Divide by $1.15$.
explanation: New price $= 115\%$ of original, so original $= \$115 \div 1.15 = \$100$.

stem: After a $12\%$ price rise, a service costs $\$280$. What was the original price (in dollars)?
hint: A $12\%$ rise means the new price is $112\%$ of the original. Divide by $1.12$.
explanation: New price $= 112\%$ of original, so original $= \$280 \div 1.12 = \$250$.

stem: A coat is reduced by $30\%$ and sells for $\$63$. What was the original price (in dollars)?
hint: A $30\%$ reduction means the sale price is $70\%$ of the original. Divide by $0.70$.
explanation: Sale price $= 70\%$ of original, so original $= \$63 \div 0.70 = \$90$.
